text: directly, and though it was a recording from two-hundred years before, the despair, desperation, and agony in the man’s expression was clear . “I want more time with Diane. I want to see my kids grow up .” The video flickered, cutting to a high-angle view of an operating room. Several individuals in green scrubs, wearing facemasks and gloves, worked around a table. A motionless, human-shaped form lay atop it, draped in a blanket. One of the surgeons stepped aside, allowing the camera view of the subject’s exposed brain . “The connections for the implant are complete,” said Doctor Yuan. One of the others wheeled a steel cart to her, and she lifted an electronic device from atop it. “Once this is inserted, we can begin the upload .” The war room was silent as the recording’s speed increased, moving the surgeons ten times faster. When it slowed to normal, there were alarms beeping in the room, and the surgeons’ voices were frantic . “He’s flatlining .” “We need the paddles — ” “No!” Doctor Yuan shouted. “They’ll fry the implant !” “We can’t just let him die, Jessica !” “He was already dead. The research is what matters now, and it’s the only chance he has of seeing his family again .” “That’s not how it’s supposed to be, Goddammit !” “If we succeed, it won’t ever have to be like this again .” “We can at least perform CPR — ” Doctor Yuan shook her head. “The implant is too delicate. We can’t risk disrupting the connection, or we might do irreversible damage to his consciousness .” “How much damage have we done, already ?” “He accepted the risks. You did, too, when you joined this project .” The continuous tone of the heart monitor punctuated her statement for six long seconds before the recording flickered again . Kevin Turner sat at a table in a nondescript room. A dozen wooden blocks painted different colors lay scattered before him. His cheeks were full, there was a healthy color to his skin, and dark hair atop his head . “I don’t—” The voice was Kevin’s, but his mouth didn’t move until he repeated the words. “I don’t want to do this anymore .” “A little at a time, Kevin,” Doctor Yuan said from off-camera. “It’s perfectly natural that things are difficult for you now. Though the ultimate functions and appearance are the same, your mind is going to take time to learn how to control your body. Please, try once more .” Frowning, Kevin raised his arm above the table. The movements were wobbly, jerky, imprecise. His brow furrowed as he reached forward, guiding his shaky hand toward one of the red blocks. Tentatively, he pinched the block between forefinger and thumb, and lifted it off the table . “Good. Would you please stack it atop one of the same color?” the Doctor asked . His eyes flicked down to the other blocks, pupils dilating and shrinking. Slowly, he moved his hand, hovering it over first a green block, and then a blue . “It’s okay if you are uncertain about the colors, Kevin. It’s not your fault,” a male said from off-camera. “We just need to know, so we can make the necessary adjustments to get you back to normal .” “Doctor Anderson is right, Kevin,” Doctor Yuan agreed. “We’ll take things one step at a time .” With a slight, unnatural nod, Kevin lowered his hand. Just before the block touched the one beneath it, his arm jerked. The blocks slammed together. The red block flew from his grasp, clattering on the floor . “Dammit!” Kevin roared through his closed mouth. He swung his arm over the surface of the table, clumsily sweeping all the blocks off. “I told you I don’t want to do this !” “It’s all right, Kevin,” Anderson said. “We can be done for today .” Another flicker, and the scene changed. Kevin paced in a carpeted room. A beige couch and two matching chairs rested nearby. A woman — blonde, somewhere in her forties, perhaps — sat in one of the chairs, wringing her hands in her lap . “Kevin, please,” she said . “Please, Diane? I don’t know what you expect. You tell me that and I’m supposed to be cool and collected after all this time ?” “Just…be patient, please. We knew this would take time, and it’s worked out better than we hoped. You’re still here , Kevin.” She pressed her hands down on her thighs and bowed her head. “We want you to come home, but the psychiatrists say you’re still…adjusting to the changes .” “They won’t let me see the kids, Diane. My fucking kids! It’s been eight months, and I haven’t seen them since I first woke up. I don’t sleep, I don’t eat, I don’t use the fucking bathroom. All I do is think about the life I’m supposed to be living .” Diane stood and approached him. She reached out and hesitantly placed a hand on his arm. Kevin jerked away from her, turning and taking several steps back. Diane’s hand fell, and her face crumpled . “I know it’s hard — ” “You know?” he demanded, whirling on her. She retreated from his advance, stumbling around one of the chairs. He grabbed it and flung it against the wall, shattering the frame. “You think you have any fucking idea what this is like ?” Diane held her hands out. “Kevin, stop! Don’t do this !” “You, too?” He dug his fingers in his neck and pulled, tearing off a chunk of synthetic skin to reveal the metal plates of his jaws and cheek. “This is what I am now! There’s no going back from it, so take a long fucking look !” “Oh my God,” Diane cried. “This isn’t you. This isn’t you! My Kevin would never —” “No, I’m not your Kevin. He died on the operating table.” He turned and stalked
